author: @JamMastaJew
text: What type of plastic? Does this work with nylon?

author: @sofa-lofa4241
text:
Nice tip Luke,
As others here have mentioned if you use a couple of hinges on the mdf sheets and attach a handle then you could build a poor man's sheet metal brake,
Ni-chrome wire also makes a quick and easy heating element if you don't have a heat gun, maybe a future project?

If I had to guess... It's an automated cat flap for a robot cat?

author: @Fess_goat_problem
text: What happened to your finger?

author: @gregorykusiak5424
text:
That was ABS or PVC plastic?
You might’ve heated both sides to make the corner...in fact, wouldn’t it be better to heat the side you’re bending in to as opposed to the outside of the corner like you did here?

author: @Scott_C
text: Great tip for clean bends in a plastic sheet.  Just remember blue text on a black background is impossible to read.

author: @barbufodor1186
text: Hi Luke a dough roller should make your life considerably better when doing the bend .

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: We do, I didn't think about that lol, great tip mate. Thanks!

author: @MDMAx
text: I'm wondering, what other figures can be shaped out of that rectangle. How close to a perfect sphere could you shape a piece of flat plastic like that?

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: That would be pretty cool to try! It is always worth ago. :)

author: @BaconbuttywithCheese
text:
Very nice.

May I suggest some nichrome wire and a couple hinged pieces of ply. Awesome results every time with the addition of a fence for perfect alignment.

author: @philoffhistree
text: while its sat on your project like that give the large flat area a heat and it will truly sit flat

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Great point, I might have to try this! Thanks mate.

author: @docink6175
text: you could make pretty much the same "jig" and have a metal brake, you just need a couple small hinges.. I do like your presser board idea

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Great idea, I never heard of a metal break until now. They look pretty cool!

author: @hkitservices
text:
Nice video, but you missed the security measures.
it is obvious that we need to use gloves to protect our hands from the heat on the soft platic & gun, but a reminder is never useless.

Apparently everybody in this workshop likes to hammer their fingers :)

author: @BushImports
text: You need to build a sheet metal brake for bending the sheet metal, you probably can mind how to here on YouTube if you don't know what one looks like or how it works. The plastic does appear to look better.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Thanks for the tip mate! I never heard of a metal break until coming into the comments. They do look pretty cool, and definitely could be built. :)

author: @NorthernKitty
text: Great job!  I'm wondering if the thumb injury was related to working with the aluminum?  That's another reason it might be better to work with plastic where you can.  😊  I can't begin to count the number of times I've been sliced up by a raw edge of aluminum.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: This has happened a few times, but this Injury is related to me not being a master chef LOL. Thanks mate. :)

replies.author: @NorthernKitty
replies.text: @MyNameIsLukey Ah, then that would be my second most frequent method of thumb/finger injuries.  😄  Good health, hope it heals quickly!! 🥰

author: @robertpitt8418
text: WHAT A GREAT IDEA - THANK YOU

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Thanks mate!!

author: @SI00000
text:
Great tip.
The electric tape covered napkin bandage on the thumb is a sign of how a true craftsman handles cuts.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Hahahaha This made me chuckle, Thanks mate!

author: @vegardliland
text: Awesome, would love to see a build of either a plastic or sheet metal bending jig! Keep up the great videos!

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Great idea! These jigs are always super useful. Thanks mate. :)

replies.author: @karlmyers6518
replies.text:
@MyNameIsLukey you were so close to a metal bending jig once it was all attached to the bench.

A couple of hinges and 45 and 90 stopper on top is exactly how I bent my lid on my video.  Sometimes metal is the only way to go.

author: @BigRich2580
text:
Another cool Video Luke, your enthusiasm is contagious, keep it going and i look forward to the next video.

What you done to your thumb buddy?

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Thanks mate! The worst part is that I didn't cut it whilst working project, I was cutting onions at home and slipped lol!

author: @dazer123
text: And Britain's best award for overacting goes to ........

replies.author: @pollywollydo
replies.text: its flat plain and simple give the lad a break🙂

replies.author: @Nick_Tag
replies.text: I always wonder what type of folks say stuff like this... it’s scientifically proven to be lardons munching wotsits in their grandma’s basement. Go outside and hug someone.

replies.author: @Nick_Tag
replies.text: Desmond Bagle works in becoming boring the way u want it maybe. Enthusiasm is infectious, that’s why nobody watches my vids cause i try to be ‘cool’ lol

replies.author: @Nick_Tag
replies.text: Desmond Bagley tl;dr ...

replies.author: @William_Hada
replies.text: Show us the link to your video where you were nominated for an Oscar! I think you are jealous of his enthusiasm and the joy he gets from building and creating.

author: @czechyorker
text: When you point a camera towards a light source and see some rays of light, it means that the camera lens is dirty. Wipe it before you start recording.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Great tip, I will keep this in mind lol. Thanks mate. :)

author: @htmagic
text: Ask your Dad to help you build a bending brake to bend aluminum. A few angle scrap and you'll get perfect bends in sheet metal.

author: @weegaz22
text: 3:12 spoiler...he's building the worlds biggest Floppy disk

replies.author: @scotttovey
replies.text: Good catch!  😎👍

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: You might be on to something! Lol :)

replies.author: @herbetone
replies.text: @MyNameIsLukey 😊🔜

author: @paulmaydaynight9925
text:
see's left thumb, that's really why you want to use plastic:-P

make a pen,fill with diluted ink, draw lines inside edge, dry, apply 12v to lines aka super localised heat, bend to angle, no custom touch pad thumb accessories required ^_~
